danger_signals.visual_form: Red leader cat (Goro) or blue underling cats (Meowky) on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Direct collision threat; loss of life if touched while cat is un-stunned

danger_signals.visual_form: Large coin with Goro's face enters from screen edge
danger_signals.meaning: Immune to all defensive mechanics (microwaves); instant death on contact even mid-air; time pressure escalation

danger_signals.visual_form: Audio alert; visual consequence: more Meowky cats spawn, all enemies accelerate
danger_signals.meaning: Player has lingered too long; danger level increases dramatically

danger_signals.visual_form: Trampoline changes color after each bounce (tracks through 4 bounces)
danger_signals.meaning: Structural integrity degradation; 5th bounce breaks it, creating fall hazard
Temporary platform failure; fall hazard for player and cats

danger_signals.visual_form: Gaps between horizontal passages; gaps bridged only by trampolines
danger_signals.meaning: Fall hazard; player loses life if falls to bottom without trampoline bounce

safety_signals.visual_form: Player character bouncing vertically on trampoline platform
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability; cats cannot harm player while bouncing up or down

safety_signals.visual_form: Flashing door releases burst of microwaves when opened by Mappy
safety_signals.meaning: Offensive tool; sweeps cats away, grants points; doubled points if Goro caught

safety_signals.visual_form: Cat briefly immobilized after door opens toward it or after microwave hit
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety window; stunned cats cannot pursue; window closes after short delay

safety_signals.visual_form: Stolen goods (stereos, TVs, paintings, computers, safes) collected by Mappy
safety_signals.meaning: Progress toward round completion; collecting pairs consecutively increases point multiplier

safety_signals.visual_form: Item location coincides with leader cat position
safety_signals.meaning: High-risk collection opportunity; briefly stuns Goro when collected, grants bonus

faction_indicators.color_identity: Primary player character (color varies by port; typically light/neutral)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Police mouse identity; hero identity communicated through role name and narrative framing
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mansion setting frames player as law enforcement recovering stolen property; Mappy operates the microwave doors (exclusive mechanic), positioning him as authority wielder

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Cat enemies; hierarchy communicated through size (Goro large) and color differentiation
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Goro: large, red, moves with leadership autonomy; Meowky: smaller, blue, act as underlings with follower behavior patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mansion hideout; stolen items scattered throughout represent criminal operation; cats' bouncing behavior on trampolines mirrors player but without breaking them, differentiating their nature

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fewer Meowky cats on screen; slower cat movement; basic platform layout
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cats move predictably; player has more reaction time; fewer simultaneous threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Additional Meowky cats enter mansion; all enemy movement visibly accelerates; more environmental complexity (attic access, bells above trampolines, vanishing floor sections)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cats swarm with increased coordination; player must employ microwave doors more defensively; risk of being cornered increases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: More Meowky cats spawn and remain on screen; audible warning cue; visual indication of accelerated enemy speed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All cats move at elevated speed; player forced into offensive/evasive strategies; round completion becomes urgent

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large Gosenzo coin with Goro's face enters from screen edge; unmissable visual indicator of maximum threat level
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Instant death on contact (no invulnerability windows); coin immune to all defensive mechanics; player must evade completely or end round immediately

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All 10 stolen items collected (visual counter or item disappearance); round transitions to bonus stage (balloon-popping challenge with vertical passages)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies cease pursuit; player enters isolated bonus environment; danger state resets for next round

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Isolated vertical passage environment; balloons distributed throughout; trampolines as primary navigation tool
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No enemy threats; pure platforming and timing challenge; player bounces and veers through passages to pop balloons

environmental_storytelling.example: Stolen items (stereos, TVs, paintings, computers, safes) scattered throughout mansion communicate ongoing theft operation; their positioning across multiple levels tells story of gang's systematic looting

Mansion setting with horizontal passages, doors, gaps, and eventually attic access frames the space as criminal hideout; physical layout communicates both escape routes for thieves and strategic chokepoints for law enforcement
Special flashing doors that release microwaves are exclusive to player (Mappy); this mechanic communicates police authority and defensive/offensive advantage over criminal cats
Cats cannot break trampolines (unlike Mappy) and cannot open microwave doors; physical differences in behavior tell story of species-based abilities and criminal limitations
Isolated balloon-popping challenge provides respite from cat pursuit; frames successful item recovery as enabling safe operations, contrasting the danger of main rounds
Color-change progression on trampolines (4 bounces before breaking) communicates infrastructure wear under repeated pressure, echoing themes of law enforcement wear-and-tear

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Follows Mappy actively; can be stunned by door opening or microwave burst; presence behind items creates high-value collection opportunity
Immobilized; vulnerable; returns to pursuit after short delay
Enters screen as large coin during time-pressure phase; unstoppable; immune to microwave defense; instant-kill on contact
Multiple underlings swarm mansion; speed increases as round progresses; more spawn when hurry-up warning triggered
Knocked back and briefly immobilized when door opens toward them; returns to pursuit after stun expires
Swept away horizontally by microwave burst; removed from screen; re-enter from top screen edge after delay
Invulnerable to cat contact; can veer left/right while ascending; cannot change direction while falling; breaks trampoline on 5th consecutive bounce
Vulnerable to cat contact; can open/close doors; can collect items; can activate microwave doors (exclusive)
Loss of life if reaches bottom of screen; indicates failed platforming

notes: Mappy communicates world-state primarily through visual color coding (red vs. blue faction), spatial mechanics (trampoline safety vs. gap danger), and progressive difficulty escalation (cat spawning, speed increase, Gosenzo coin). The microwave door mechanic serves as visual/mechanical expression of player authority. Danger increases through cumulative visual signals: cat proliferation, animation speed increases, and introduction of new hazards (vanishing floors, bells, attic access). Safety is communicated inversely: trampoline bouncing invulnerability, microwave effectiveness, and stunned enemy states. The political situation (police mouse vs. thieving cats) is communicated through role identity, exclusive mechanics (microwave doors), and environmental framing (mansion as criminal hideout, player as recovery agent).
